channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: U.S. falls to Belgium, 4-1, ending tantalizing World Cup run with defensive miscues
item.description: The Red Devils ended the Americans’ captivating run through the tournament in the round of 16, denying them their first trip to the World Cup quarterfinals since 2002. Belgium advanced to face Spain in Friday’s quarterfinal in Inglewood, California.

item.title: Woman accuses Graham Platner, Democratic Senate nominee in Maine, of sexually assaulting her
item.description: A woman who said she previously dated Platner, the 41-year-old ex-Marine and Democratic nominee in Maine, alleged in interviews that he sexually assaulted her in her home near the end of 2021. In a video posted on X on after the allegation surfaced, Platner denied wrongdoing but said “we are taking the time to reflect on the best path forward for the state that I love, the people that I love, the movement I belong to, and the goal of defeating Susan Collins.”

item.title: Our reporter tried Gwyneth Paltrow’s Goop Kitchen for a week. Here’s what happened.
item.description: Ashley Fetters Maloy writes that eating Goop’s grub left her feeling clean, powerful, smug and exasperated.

item.title: What actually causes cancer, and why has it become so common? An oncologist explains.
item.description: While more people are being diagnosed with cancer today compared with 50 years ago, the good news is that fewer people are dying of it.

item.title: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
item.description: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

item.title: After he wrote a scathing message to ICE, federal agents showed up at his door. Now he’s suing.
item.description: David Streever of Rochester, New York, sued Department of Homeland Security officials on Monday, saying the agency’s actions violated his First Amendment rights.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Tell kids that ex’s betrayal means ‘I no longer believe in love’?
item.description: A divorced letter writer doesn’t want to lie to the kids when they ask how things are with a new girlfriend.

item.title: Trump rushes White House helipad project ahead of visit by China’s president, adding $875,000 and requiring 24/7 work, records show
item.description: The contractor has sought to shave off more than a month of the planned construction timeline, documents show.

item.title: Trump says he asked FIFA’s president to review U.S. star’s World Cup suspension but ‘didn’t tell him what to do’
item.description: President Donald Trump’s involvement in obtaining a reprieve for Folarin Balogun has fueled concerns about political influence and cast a pall over Monday night’s match against Belgium.

item.title: White House report accuses Smithsonian museum of ‘extreme political activism’ and erasing nation’s heritage
item.description: The 162-page report on the National Museum of American History marks an escalation of President Donald Trump’s criticism of the Smithsonian museums.

item.title: Europe wants tourists out — except this kind
item.description: Restrictions on short-term rentals and other limitations are turning international travel into a luxury.

item.title: An NHL player stars in today’s On the Record
item.description: An NHL player stars in today’s On the Record

item.title: July Fourth. Ali Khamenei. World Cup. Tax law. Marine heat. Cancer rates. Goop Kitchen. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump says he overruled plan to cancel July Fourth celebration on Mall amid weather evacuations
item.description: The chaotic scramble for shelter at the event marking America’s 250th raised questions about whether officials had adequately prepared for severe heat and threatened storms. A senior White House official said Sunday that “all the entities involved” had recommended calling the festivities off.

item.title: The 42 definitive rules for a group vacation rental
item.description: A handbook for staying friends by the end of the trip, from planning to checking out.

item.title: Trump urged FIFA to review the suspension of Folarin Balogun before the U.S. star was cleared to play in Monday’s World Cup game
item.description: President Donald Trump personally spoke with FIFA President Gianni Infantino, a person familiar with the matter said. The decision by the global soccer governing body on the eve of the United States’ round-of-16 match against Belgium caused an immediate stir.

item.title: Divorced and middle-aged, he needed to make a friend. But he’d forgotten how.
item.description: Speed-friending events are helping men fill a relationship void that is contributing to a national epidemic of loneliness.

item.title: U.S. top scorer Folarin Balogun can play in Monday’s World Cup showdown against Belgium after FIFA suspends his one-game ban
item.description: The striker’s red card against Bosnia had drawn the ire of U.S. fans and sparked debate among soccer analysts.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Chilly relationship bugs stepmom, but she’s wrong about its source
item.description: The letter writer was deeply affected by Dad’s abrupt transitions after Mom’s death but “can’t tell” the stepmother this.

item.title: The Pacific Ocean is running a fever, an ominous sign for weather around the world in the coming weeks and months
item.description: A marine heat wave covering an area eight times the size of the United States could soon fuel serious storms and extreme heat.

item.title: Historian Tom Holland sees echoes of Rome in the American experiment
item.description: A conversation with the British author, podcaster and amateur cricketer on the United States at 250.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Trump fashions America’s 250th anniversary in his own image, blending patriotic spectacle with campaign-style politics
item.description: Nearly every aspect of the celebration in Washington bore President Donald Trump’s imprint, as decisions he made transformed an official commemoration of American history into another polarizing moment in American politics.

item.title: Secret Service officials order thousands to evacuate the Mall amid severe weather warning, prompting scramble for shelter
item.description: The order came hours after hot weather forced the cancellation of an Independence Day parade through Washington.

item.title: How Taylor Swift pulled off the wedding she wants us to see
item.description: News of the closest thing America has to a royal wedding was both everywhere and unknowable.

item.title: Celebrations for America’s 250th beset by extreme heat and political division
item.description: In the nation’s sweltering capital, thousands celebrate America's landmark birthday as heavy security, delays and extremists stoke frustration.

item.title: 25 reasons to be optimistic about America’s future
item.description: Freedom and prosperity are never guaranteed, but it’s a mistake to bet against America. That’s as true now as when the country was founded 250 years ago, the Editorial Board writes.

item.title: 6 surprising tips for how to hydrate better
item.description: Experts share the latest thinking on how to make sure you’re consuming the water your body needs while avoiding health risks.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Okay to nag a huge favor out of mom, to help fretful mother-in-law?
item.description: Knowing it’s inconvenient, letter writer may summon mom’s boyfriend to their beach house to calm “nervous wreck” mother-in-law.

item.title: Each American flag starts with thread. Here’s how they’re made.
item.description: Behind every flag made at South Carolina's Valley Forge Flag Co. is a carefully choreographed process.

item.title: In Tehran, huge throngs mourn supreme leader Ali Khamenei, killed in an airstrike on first day of war
item.description: The public display of lamentation and bereavement for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed in a February airstrike, was an opportunity for collective closure after some of the most perilous months in the Islamic republic’s history, but also a forceful show of defiance from a system badly battered but very much still standing.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: Keyword, a new game from The Washington Post, gives you six words — each missing one letter — and you have to find the word that connects them.

item.title: ‘JustT&T Married!’: Everything we know about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's star-studded wedding
item.description: A representative confirmed to media outlets that the pair had wed, as giant purple signs outside the Madison Square Garden event blared: “JustT&T Married!” Neither Swift nor Kelce have publicly confirmed the marriage.

item.title: 50 years ago, The Post pictured life in 2026. Here’s what it got right.
item.description: Science editor Thomas O’Toole forecast innovations, including gene editing, deep-sea mining and nuclear-powered artificial hearts.

item.title: 7 foot symptoms that might be the first signs of a hidden condition
item.description: These strange issues may seem minor, but they can signal problems elsewhere in the body.

item.title: This mistake helped transform 22-year-old George Washington into a leader
item.description: The lesson of George Washington’s life is not that leadership qualities are innate, but that they are acquired the hard way.

item.title: Heat causes Great American State Fair to close temporarily and forces other disruptions
item.description: Temperatures over the July Fourth weekend are expected to exceed 100 degrees. The D.C. fire department told The Washington Post that it had treated people at the fair for heat-related illnesses Friday.

item.title: You can’t just tell your bestie to be nicer to her baby. Can you?
item.description: Letter writer worries friends are making parenthood — and baby’s life — harder with constant jokes that he’s a “terrorist.”

item.title: We asked 25 Americans how they feel about their country. Here’s what they said.
item.description: On the National Mall, in the heart of Washington, D.C., Americans expressed their pride, concern and hopes for the country as it turns 250 years old.

item.title: Annual Capitol concert to go forward tonight, despite concerns about the extreme heat, but gates open later
item.description: The "Capitol Fourth Concert" will still begin at 8 p.m., but the public gates will not open until 7 p.m. because of the heat. Temperatures today and Saturday are expected to exceed 100 degrees.

item.title: JD Vance makes the case for a more Christian America
item.description: Secularism has divided the nation, the vice president says in an interview.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Take today’s news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: Temperatures are soaring across much of the country. See where Americans lack air conditioning.
item.description: A Post analysis found about 3 million U.S. households lack a cooling system in regions where the weekend's temperatures will be particularly intense, with residents facing "major" or "extreme" heat risk.

item.title: Iran and Israel. Reflecting Pool. Freedom 250. Trans athletes. Heat dome. Safer cannabis. Swift-Kelce wedding. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: U.S. warned Iran about possible Israeli efforts to kill top negotiators in ceasefire talks
item.description: Washington’s objection to killing Abbas Araghchi, Iran’s foreign minister, and Mohammad Ghalibaf, the country’s parliamentary speaker, was so acute that this spring the U.S. took the extraordinary step of asking intermediaries to warn Iran about Israel’s assassination aims, officials said.

item.title: Trump's proposal to ease rules on mailing guns could benefit a company in which his son owns a stake
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is a key player in GrabAGun, a company that hopes to dominate internet firearms sales.

item.title: 10 ways to protect yourself during the July Fourth heat wave
item.description: 10 ways to protect yourself during the July Fourth heat wave

item.title: Public banned from ‘A Capitol Fourth’ concert rehearsal over heat safety concerns, and Friday’s show could be canceled
item.description: With temperatures exceeding 100 degrees, U.S. Capitol Police closed the Thursday evening rehearsal for the annual outdoor concert.

item.title: Former Olympian indicted after arrest over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
item.description: David Hearn has denied misconduct, saying he reached into the water after noticing a piece of the pool’s blue liner had become partially detached.

item.title: Capitals star Alex Ovechkin, NHL’s all-time goals leader, will come back to Washington after weighing retirement
item.description: The 40-year-old captain entered the offseason facing the end of his contract.
